turns.content: I keep thinking that adding an interceptor in Tonic is just like wrapping a function with middleware in Go, but I'm confused about how to actually inject it into the service. Does tonic::interceptor::Interceptor wrap the entire request handler automatically?

That's a common mental model, but Tonic interceptors don't wrap handlers like Go middleware; they sit in the call chain before the server is actually invoked. You implement your own struct that implements tonic::interceptor::Interceptor and then pass it to the service builder via the .with_interceptors() method on ServiceBuilder. The interceptor runs for every request before any actual service logic executes, so you're not just wrapping a function but replacing how requests are processed at the transport level.
Okay, so if I want to read headers and inject auth tokens, do I need to manually parse the grpc::Metadata object in my interceptor? Is there a trait that makes this easier than what I'm doing?

Yes, you absolutely need to work with grpc::Metadata directly inside your Interceptor implementation. The tonic crate provides an impl for the metadata type so you can call .iter() or .get() on it within your interceptor's intercept method. There isn't a separate trait that auto-parses headers; you must access the Metadata field in the request context via the grpc::metadata variable provided by Tonic when calling intercept(). This is where you'd extract the authorization header and pass it to your auth service.
